volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Merchant (Section 4.5.812b - Section 5.2.890)

sections.label: Section 4.5.812b
sections.translation: That very pretty father of yours has brought a mistress into the house .

sections.label: Section 4.5.813b
sections.translation: How say you ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.814
sections.original: Adveniens mater rure eam offendit domi .
sections.translation: Your mother , on arriving from the country , found her at home .

sections.label: Section 4.5.815
sections.original: Pol haud censebam istarum esse operarum patrem .
sections.translation: By my troth , I didn ’ t think my father was a person for those practices . Is the woman now even still in-doors ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.816b
sections.translation: Even still .

sections.label: Section 4.5.816c
sections.translation: Do you follow me .

sections.label: Section 4.6.817
sections.translation: I ’ faith , the women do live upon hard terms , and , wretched creatures , on much more unjust ones than the men . For if a husband has been keeping a mistress without the knowledge of his wife ,

sections.label: Section 4.6.820
sections.original: id si rescivit uxor , impunest viro ;
sections.translation: if the wife comes to know it , the husband gets off with impunity ; if , unknown to the husband , the wife goes from the house out of doors , a pretext arises for the husband , the marriage is dissolved [*] . I wish the law was the same for the husband as for the wife ; for the wife that is a good one , is content with one husband ;

sections.label: Section 4.6.825
sections.original: qui minus vir una uxore contentus siet ?
sections.translation: why , any the less , should the husband be content with one wife ? By my troth , I ’ d give cause , if men were punished in the same way ( if any one should be keeping a mistress unknown to his wife ), as those women are repudiated who are guilty of a slip , that there should be more divorced men than there are women now .

sections.label: Section 5.1.830
sections.original: Límen superum ínferumque , salve , simul autem vale :
sections.translation: O higher and lower [*] portions of the threshold , now both of you farewell . This day for the last time do I raise this foot within my father ’ s house . The ease , the enjoyment , the in-dwelling , the habitation of this house is henceforth for me cut off , destroyed , and alienated . I am undone ! The household Gods of my parents , the Lar the father of the family [*] ,

sections.label: Section 5.1.835
sections.original: vobis mando , meum parentum rem bene ut tutemini .
sections.translation: to you do I recommend , that you will kindly protect the possessions of my parents . I shall now seek other household Gods for myself , another Lar , another city , another state . The people of Attica I do detest ; for where worse manners are on the increase every day , where , those who are friends , those who are faithless , you are not able , to distinguish ,

sections.label: Section 5.1.840
sections.original: ubique id eripiatur , animo tuo quod placeat maxume ,
sections.translation: and where that is torn away from you , which especially pleases your taste , there , in fact , if a kingdom were given one , that country is not desirable .

sections.label: Section 5.2.842
sections.original: Divom atque hominum quae spectatrix atque era eadem es hominibus ,
sections.translation: Thou who art the overlooker of Gods and of men , and the mistress of mortals as well , inasmuch as thou hast indulged me in this hope that I entertained , I do return thee thanks . What Deity is there now that is joyous with gladness like mine ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.845
sections.original: domi erát quod quaeritabam : sex sodales repperi ,
sections.translation: That was at home which I was in search of . There did I find six companions , life , friendship , my native land , festivity , mirth , and jollity . On finding these , at the same moment did I utterly destroy ten very bad things , wrath , hatred , folly , ruin , perverseness , grief , tears , exile , want , and loneliness .

sections.label: Section 5.2.850
sections.original: date , di , quaeso conveniundi mi eius celerem copiam .
sections.translation: Ye Gods , I pray you grant me a speedy opportunity of meeting him .

sections.label: Section 5.2.851
sections.original: Apparatus sum ut videtis : abicio superbiam ;
sections.translation: I ’ m ready prepared , as you see . Pride I cast aside ; I ’ m my own companion , attendant , horse , groom , esquire ; I ’ m my own master , I , too , obey myself ; for my own self do I carry what I require . O Cupid ! how powerful art thou .

sections.label: Section 5.2.855
sections.original: nam tu quemvis confidentem facile tuis factis facis ,
sections.translation: For easily dost thou render any one resolute through thy deeds , and then again , the same person diffident forthwith from being over bold .

sections.label: Section 5.2.857
sections.original: Cogito quonam égo illum curram quaeritatum .
sections.translation: I ’ m thinking which way to run in search of him .

sections.label: Section 5.2.857b
sections.translation: The matter ’ s resolved upon , that I ’ ll seek her everywhere , wherever in the world she has been carried off from hence ; and neither shall any river stand in my way , nor mountain , nor the sea ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.860
sections.original: nec calor nec frigus metuo neque ventum neque grandinem ;
sections.translation: indeed , nor heat , nor cold ; I dread neither wind nor hail ; the torrents of rain I ’ ll submit to ; labour , heat , and thirst , will I endure . I ’ ll neither stop nor rest anywhere at night , or in the day , assuredly , before I shall have met with either my mistress or my death .

sections.label: Section 5.2.864
sections.original: Nescio quóia vox ad aures mi advolavit .
sections.translation: Some voice , I know not who ’ s , flew to my ear .

sections.label: Section 5.2.864b
sections.translation: You do I invoke ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.865
sections.original: vos , Lares viales , ut me bene tutetis .
sections.translation: ye Lares of the roads [*] , that you will kindly lend me aid .

sections.label: Section 5.2.865b
sections.translation: Jupiter ! isn ’ t that Charinus ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.866b
sections.translation: Fellow-citizens , fare ye well .

sections.label: Section 5.2.866c
sections.translation: Charinus , stop , this instant .

sections.label: Section 5.2.867b
sections.translation: Who calls me back ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.867c
sections.translation: Hope , Safety , Victory .

sections.label: Section 5.2.868
sections.translation: What do you want with me ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.868b
sections.translation: To go along with you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.868c
sections.translation: Look for another companion ; these companions that have possession of me , will not part with me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.869b
sections.translation: Who are they ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.870
sections.translation: Care , misery , sickness , tears , and lamentation .

sections.label: Section 5.2.871
sections.original: Repudia istos comites atque hoc respice et revortere .
sections.translation: Drive away those companions , and look this way and return .

sections.label: Section 5.2.872
sections.translation: If indeed you wish to speak to me , do you follow .

sections.label: Section 5.2.872b
sections.translation: Stop , this instant !

sections.label: Section 5.2.873
sections.original: Male facis , properantem qui me commorare . sol abit .
sections.translation: You do amiss , in delaying me as I haste ; the sun is setting .

sections.label: Section 5.2.874
sections.original: Si huc item properes ut istuc properas , facias rectius :
sections.translation: If you would make haste in this direction , just as you are hastening in that one , you ’ d be doing more rightly ;

sections.label: Section 5.2.875
sections.original: huc secundus ventus nunc est ; cape modo vorsoriam :
sections.translation: this way there is now a prospering gale , only tack about . Here is a fair Westerly breeze ; there is a showery Southern blast . The one causes a calm ; the other stirs up all the waves . Betake yourself towards the land , Charinus , in this direction . Don ’ t you see right opposite ? Black clouds and showers are coming on . Look now to the left ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.880
sections.original: caelum ut est splendore plenum atque ut dei is tuc vorti iubent ?
sections.translation: how full the heaven is of brightness . Don ’ t you see right opposite ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.881
sections.original: Religionem illic mi obiecit : recipiam me illuc .
sections.translation: He has thrown religious scruples [*] in my way ; I ’ ll betake myself in that direction .

sections.label: Section 5.2.881b
sections.translation: You are wise . O Charinus , turn your steps , and turn your feet as well , in the opposite direction .

sections.label: Section 5.2.883b
sections.translation: Extend your arm . Catch hold of me . Do you hold me new ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.883c
sections.translation: I ’ m holding you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.883d
sections.translation: Hold on , then .

sections.label: Section 5.2.884
sections.translation: Whither now were you going ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.884b
sections.translation: Into banishment .

sections.label: Section 5.2.884c
sections.translation: What to do there ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.884d
sections.translation: As a wretched person would .

sections.label: Section 5.2.885
sections.original: Ne pave , restituam iam ego te in gaudio antiquo ut sies .
sections.translation: Don ’ t fear ; this instant shall I restore you to joyousness before you go away .

sections.label: Section 5.2.886
sections.original: maxime quod vis audire , id audies , quod gaudeas
sections.translation: A thing that you especially long to hear , the same shall you hear for you to rejoice at . Stay this instant ; I ’ m come as a friend , full of the kindest feelings .

sections.label: Section 5.2.888
sections.translation: Your mistress —

sections.label: Section 5.2.888b
sections.translation: What of her ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.888c
sections.translation: I know where she is .

sections.label: Section 5.2.888d
sections.translation: Prithee , do you ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.889
sections.translation: She ’ s safe and sound .

sections.label: Section 5.2.889b
sections.translation: Where is she safe ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.889c
sections.translation: I know where .

sections.label: Section 5.2.889d
sections.translation: I ’ d much rather I did .

sections.label: Section 5.2.890
sections.translation: Can ’ t you possibly be calm in your feelings ?
